steps.title: Find the new angle
steps.content:
Point $A$ starts at the top of the unit circle, which is angle $\frac{\pi}{2}$. Rotate counterclockwise by $\frac{2\pi}{3}$.
$$\frac{\pi}{2}+\frac{2\pi}{3}=\frac{3\pi}{6}+\frac{4\pi}{6}=\frac{7\pi}{6}$$

steps.title: Use unit circle coordinates
steps.content:
At angle $\frac{7\pi}{6}$, both coordinates are negative.
$$\cos(\frac{7\pi}{6})=-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$
$$\sin(\frac{7\pi}{6})=-\frac{1}{2}$$
So $P=(-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2},-\frac{1}{2})$. The answer is D.
